name: Farefare (Frafra)
notes: Farefare / Frafra (farefare), a Gur (Mabia) language of the Upper East Region of Ghana and southern Burkina Faso. STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Script: Latin alphabet, with the additional vowel letters ⟨ɛ ɔ ɩ ʋ⟩ typical of Ghanaian orthographies. A full spec must model ATR vowel harmony, nasal vowels and lexical tone (none of which is written), and the orthography is not fully standardised across the Gurenɛ/Nankani/Booni varieties. Wikidata gives no ISO 639-3 or Glottolog code for the Wikimedia code `gur` as such (the Wikidata item is the Farefare Wikipedia); the Glottolog/ISO code for the language itself (`gur`/`gure1255` family names collide with the Gur family label) was not verified, so both fields are left null rather than guessed. Input is assumed to be the Latin Farefare orthography. wikidata_qid left unset: the code's Wikidata row resolves to the Wikimedia project item, not the language item; not recorded rather than record a wrong id.

ancestors.notes: Atlantic-Congo — the classification node this language sits under.
